data.title: Confluent to announce Q4 and FY 2025 financial results on Feb 11, 2026 after market close. No conference call due to pending IBM transaction.
data.description: A technology company based in Mountain View, California, Confluent, Inc. (NASDAQ: CFLT), a leader in data streaming, is set to unveil its financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, concluding on December 31, 2025, post the U.S. market closure on February 11, 2026. Due to the impending deal with IBM, there will be no earnings conference call or provision of a financial forecast. Confluent offers a cutting-edge data streaming platform.

data.title: Palantir to release Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 2, 2026 after US markets close. Webcast at 3:00 PM MT / 5:00 PM ET.
data.description: Denver-based Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) is set to unveil its Q4 and fiscal year 2025 performance numbers on February 2, 2026, post the U.S. markets' closure. The company will conduct a webcast discussing the financials at 3:00 PM MT / 5:00 PM ET. Interested parties can access the live webcast and replay at investors.palantir.com. Shareholders have the option to submit and vote on questions by visiting the platform.

data.title: Upstart, based in San Mateo, to reveal Q4 and FY 2025 results on Feb 10, 2026, via a Business Wire report.
data.description: A company based in San Mateo, California is set to reveal its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on February 10, 2026. This announcement will include key figures regarding the company's revenue, profits, and overall growth during that period.

data.title: Five9 and Google Cloud partner on Enterprise CX AI solution for large enterprises for seamless, AI-driven customer experiences.
data.description: In San Ramon, California, Five9 (Nasdaq: FIVN) revealed an enhanced collaboration with Google Cloud, unveiling a novel Enterprise CX AI solution for large enterprises. This alliance aims to facilitate superior customer connections for businesses, enabling them to provide sophisticated, seamless, and AI-powered customer interactions. The joint offering merges Five9's AI-Infused Intelligent CX Platform with Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience (GECX) and cutting-edge AI capabilities, including Google’s Gemini models.

data.title: Paul Fipps joins NetApp's Board of Directors, bringing 20 years of experience. The board now has 10 directors, 9 independent.
data.description: NetApp revealed that Paul Fipps, the President of Global Customer Operations at ServiceNow, has been appointed to their Board of Directors. This decision brings the total number of directors on the board to ten, with nine being independent. Notably, half of the board members have been appointed in the last five years. Fipps, who boasts over 20 years of experience in fostering technology-driven expansion and customer evolution, will bring valuable expertise to his new role. Currently at ServiceNow, he is responsible for overseeing global customer operations.

data.title: Sonos appoints 3 new directors (Carmine Arabia, Mandy Fields, Joe Kennedy) to Board of Directors for next phase of growth.
data.description: In Santa Barbara, California, Sonos, Inc. (Nasdaq: SONO) has named Carmine Arabia, Mandy Fields, and Joe Kennedy to its Board of Directors. These appointments enhance the Board's capabilities with expertise in operations, finance, and technology platforms, aligning with Sonos' strategic growth phase. The new members aim to enhance customer satisfaction and drive sustainable, long-term value for shareholders, according to Juli.

data.title: Elevra Lithium expedites NAL Expansion in N. America to boost production, cut costs. ASX:ELV; NASDAQ:ELVR; OTCQB:SYAXF.
data.description: In Brisbane, Australia, on January 12, 2026, Elevra Lithium Limited, a North American lithium producer listed as ASX:ELV, NASDAQ:ELVR, and OTCQB:SYAXF, has outlined plans for the expansion of the NAL mine. The company's refined strategy aims to expedite production growth by streamlining the permitting, construction, and capital investment processes, ultimately leading to increased output at reduced operational expenses.

data.title: Agilysys to release Q3 2026 results on Jan 26th, leading global hospitality software provider (AGYS), details in call and webcast.
data.description: Agilysys, Inc., a prominent provider of hospitality software solutions worldwide, including property management systems, point-of-sale solutions, and food-and-beverage inventory and procurement systems, will unveil its fiscal Q3 2026 results on January 26, 2026, after the market closes. The company, listed as AGYS, is known for enabling organizations to exceed the capabilities of traditional PMS. Following the release, Agilysys will conduct a conference call and webcast.

data.title: SS&C will announce Q4 and full year 2025 earnings soon.
data.description: In the latest report, it was revealed that 4 new cases of the virus have been confirmed in the region, bringing the total number of infected individuals to 37. Additionally, authorities have administered over 500 tests in the past week, with the majority of results coming back negative. The local hospital has treated 12 patients for symptoms related to the virus, and so far, 3 individuals have fully recovered. There are currently 22 active cases being monitored, and efforts to contain the spread of the virus are ongoing. The community has been urged to practice social distancing and follow strict hygiene measures to prevent further transmission.

data.title: PagerDuty names Chris Ferro as Chief Legal Officer overseeing all legal functions to advance business objectives.
data.description: PagerDuty, Inc. welcomed Chris Ferro as their new Chief Legal Officer. Ferro, who has joined the company, will be in charge of all legal operations, including corporate issues, compliance, employment, and product matters. His primary focus will be to help advance business goals while managing legal and regulatory risks effectively. Jennifer Tejada, the Chairperson and CEO of PagerDuty, expressed excitement about Ferro's appointment to the position. Ferro's role will involve overseeing all legal functions and ensuring the company's legal compliance and risk mitigation.

data.title: Avadel Pharmaceuticals shareholders approved acquisition by Alkermes. Transaction valued at $100 million.
data.description: This information is restricted from being released, published, or distributed either fully or partially in any jurisdiction where doing so would breach the laws or regulations in place in that jurisdiction.

data.title: Devon Energy to report Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 17 after US market closes, on their website.
data.description: Devon Energy Corp. (NYSE: DVN) revealed plans to unveil its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 17, 2026, after the closure of U.S. financial markets. Interested parties can access the earnings release and presentation on the company's official website, www.devonenergy.com.

data.title: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals grants 37,575 stock options and 25,525 RSUs to 10 new employees in Jan 2026.
data.description: On January 10, 2026, Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: CRNX) disclosed that it awarded non-executive employees with 37,575 stock options to buy its common stock and 25,525 restricted stock unit (“RSU”) awards under the 2021 Employment Inducement Incentive Award Plan. These awards were granted as inducements for new employees joining the company, following Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

data.title: Nasdaq reports 15,183,396,236 shares in short interest for 3,498 stocks by Dec 31, 2025, down from 15,012,659,234 shares on Dec 15, 2025.
data.description: In New York on January 12, 2026, it was reported that as of the settlement date on December 31, 2025, the short interest in 3,498 Nasdaq Global Market SM securities had reached 15,183,396,236 shares. This figure marks an increase from the previous settlement date of December 15, 2025, where short interest in 3,486 Global Market issues totaled 15,012,659,234 shares. The short interest for December translated to 2.83 days, showing a slight decrease from the previous reporting period which stood at 2.90 days.

data.title: BCP Investment Corp to release Q4 and full year 2025 earnings with call on March 6, 2026, 10 am ET.
data.description: A conference call is set to take place at 10:00 am Eastern Time on Friday, March 6, 2026.

data.title: DISH Wireless fails to pay Crown Castle, owes over $3.5 billion as contract terminated.
data.description: Crown Castle has opted to terminate its contract with DISH, putting the latter in a position where it is required to pay Crown over $3.5 billion as part of this agreement.

data.title: Wealthfront's fiscal Q3 2026: Record revenue $93.2M, net income $30.9M, platform assets $92.8B, EBITDA $43.8M.
data.description: The company experienced a significant boost in revenue, reaching a new high of $93.2 million, reflecting a 16% increase. Net income also saw a substantial rise, reaching $30.9 million, resulting in a net income margin of 33%. The total platform assets reached a record $92.8 billion, marking a substantial 21% increase. Adjusted EBITDA saw a notable growth of 24%, amounting to $43.8 million, with an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 47%.

data.title: Kenneth G. St. Romain retires as Senior VP at Pool Corporation, John B. Watwood appointed as Executive VP.
data.description: In Covington, Louisiana, on January 12, 2026, Pool Corporation revealed that Kenneth “Kenny” G. St. Romain, the Senior Vice President, is set to retire from his role later this year. Concurrently, John B. Watwood has been welcomed as the new Executive Vice President of the company. Mr. Watwood will be in charge of the North American swimming pool operations and will also supervise digital and technology projects. Reporting to Peter D. Arvan, the President and CEO of Pool Corporation, Mr. St. Romain will aid in the transition until his departure. Pool Corporation is listed on Nasdaq/GSM with the ticker symbol POOL.
SuRo Capital Corp. announces its latest investment portfolio update for Q4 and FY2025.

GERMAN FINANCE MINISTER LARS KLINGBEIL SAID EUROPE MUST NOT BE “NAIVE” ABOUT WIDENING RIFTS WITH THE U.S. AND WARNED THAT TRANSATLANTIC DIFFERENCES COULD HARM GLOBAL STABILITY AS LEADERS DISCUSS SUPPLY CHAINS, RAW MATERIALS AND CENTRAL BANK INDEPENDENCE IN WASHINGTON.
